purpose: Persistent fail-closed ledger for every defect reported during natural Fallout: New Vegas play. Chat recollection is never used as the source of truth.

policy.intake: Every newly reported or automatically detected defect is added before implementation moves on.
policy.closure: A row closes only with a focused test, a natural-play reproduction, a retail differential, promoted binary/profile provenance, and a regression test or invariant.
policy.rendererInvariant: Character, creature, AI, dialogue, quest, UI, audio, or combat work may not alter static-world material routing, transparency, depth, reflection, LOD, weather, interior lighting, HUD masking, or VR compositor behavior unless the row explicitly owns that renderer change.
policy.baselineRule: The initial known-good OpenMW/OpenMW-VR set-piece rendering lineage must be hash-pinned. Until that commit and profile are pinned, all world-render rows remain open.
policy.sliceRule: Every accepted repair is committed as a bounded slice and records its commit in this ledger.
policy.percentageRule: Open, unverified, intermittent, or synthetic-only rows earn zero parity credit. Percentages are generated from evidence, never estimated from effort.

baseline.description: Initial OpenMW/OpenMW-VR FNV import where authored terrain, buildings, interiors, clutter, furniture, windows, and ordinary set pieces rendered without the later reflection/transparency regressions.
baseline.engineCommitSubject: Apply retail FaceGen layers to FNV exposed skin
baseline.correlation: The pinned revision and binary were logged at 20:54 local, bracketing the 20:04 user recording before the next engine animation commits. Profile provenance still requires recovery, so this is a bounded visual baseline rather than certification.

defects.title: Reflection shader bleeds onto non-window world materials
symptoms: World surfaces mirror the surrounding environment
A window reflection treatment appears on unrelated meshes
defects.requiredGate: Paired baseline/current material and screenshot census across Goodsprings exterior and three interiors proves reflection only on retail-authored reflective surfaces.

defects.title: Buildings, floors, rocks, furniture, boxes, and chairs blink or disappear
symptoms: Geometry becomes transparent or vanishes by view/cell
Layer ordering allows seeing through authored set pieces
Doc Mitchell's house and floors can nearly disappear
defects.requiredGate: Camera-path screenshot and draw-state differential has zero unexplained visibility, depth-write, alpha-test, blend, cull, or render-bin changes from the known-good baseline.

defects.title: HUD quad masks or removes world geometry
symptoms: World content disappears behind or around the HUD quad
defects.requiredGate: Flat and VR HUD-on/HUD-off pixel differential proves the HUD changes only HUD pixels and never world depth or material state.

defects.title: Lighting and image space swing between black-and-white, muddy, overbright, and nuclear-winter states
symptoms: Interior entry can become black and white
Night and Strip transitions become muddy or extremely bright
Exposure changes make the world unreadable
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW time-and-weather matrix plus interior/exterior door loop matches weather, image-space, fog, ambient, sun, exposure, and transition state without synthetic overrides.

defects.title: Sky and clouds intermittently disappear
symptoms: Clouds are present in some runs or cells and absent in others
Sky was previously fixed and regressed
defects.requiredGate: Natural load, door-return, save-load, and 24-hour weather matrix has continuous retail-authored sky/cloud geometry and textures in flat and VR.

defects.title: Goodsprings flag appears duplicated and one copy does not animate
symptoms: Two flags occupy the authored location
One flag is static
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW node census and timed transform trace match instance count and controller motion.

defects.title: Town actors warp instead of naturally executing packages
symptoms: Actors jump or warp along chores
Package movement does not produce continuous locomotion
People mill around without retail timing or routes
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW lockstep traces package selection, procedure state, nav path, transform, animation group, root motion, and destination through complete natural Goodsprings schedules.

defects.title: Chore actors do not hold or use authored brooms, rakes, and equipment
symptoms: Actors attempt chores empty-handed
Equipment state and animation do not agree
defects.requiredGate: Package-to-idle-to-equipped-item differential matches retail attachment, grip, animation event, and persistence state.

defects.title: Easy Pete and Doc Mitchell do not sit correctly
symptoms: Actors stand instead of using authored furniture
Prior sit attempts were misaligned or malformed
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW furniture reservation, entry, loop, exit, transform, IK, and save-load trace matches for every Goodsprings furniture package.

defects.title: Actors and creatures float above the floor or ground
symptoms: Saloon settlers float
Many hostile creatures are off the ground
Authored positions look correct but vertical placement is wrong
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW actor root, collision foot plane, navmesh height, skeleton bound, and animation root differential stays within authored tolerance across representative races and creatures.

defects.title: A subset of Deathclaws idle tipped onto their left side
symptoms: Most Deathclaws stand correctly
Three or four enter a left-flipped idle pose
defects.requiredGate: Per-instance retail/OpenMW trace proves skeleton variant, selected group, KF root transform, blend source, root basis, and world transform for every loaded Deathclaw; no mixed orientation result is allowed.

defects.title: Speech moves jaw or mouth but leaves the rest of the face rigid
symptoms: Teeth and mouth move
Cheeks, brows, eyes, and expression remain static
Quality varies by NPC
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW dialogue trace correlates LIP phonemes, HeadAnims tracks, TRI morph weights, expression state, eye/head controllers, and rendered vertex deltas for every Goodsprings speaker.
currentFindings: The official Easy Pete voice LIP contains nonzero phoneme, brow, blink, look, squint, and head channels; the facial data is not absent.
The official HeadOld TRI exposes authored Anger, Disgust, Fear, Happy, Sad, Surprise, Mood*, Pained, CombatAnger, brow, phoneme, blink, squint, and look morphs.

FNV INFO responses already parse emotion type/value, use-emotion-animation flag, speaker animation, and listener animation, but executeEsm4Topic currently reduces the response sequence to voice filenames.
The renderer currently combines LIP values with HeadAnims tracks from active body animation KFs; it has no surviving per-response emotion state after dialogue selection.
defects.implementationContract: Carry each ordered INFO response's exact emotion type/value/flags and speaker/listener animation beside its voice, apply the authored expression/mood target with the LIP timeline and exact TRI-name aliases, drive eye/head targets through their native controllers, then clear or advance the state exactly with the response sequence. Do not invent a generic talking face.

defects.title: Dialogue actors do not turn or look toward the player
symptoms: Actors speak while facing away
No reliable eye/head/body attention transition
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW dialogue begin/end trace matches look-at target, head tracking, eye tracking, body turn threshold, and restoration to package state.

defects.title: Easy Pete and Doc Mitchell hair is malformed
symptoms: Hair basis, placement, or skinning visibly differs from retail
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW face/hair attachment and vertex/bounds differential passes for every Goodsprings NPC, including hat/no-hat states.

defects.title: Hostile actors react but remain stationary or warp in place
symptoms: Enemies acquire hostility and turn
They do not pursue or attack
Mobs animate or warp without traversing routes
defects.requiredGate: Natural combat differential matches detection, target selection, combat package, nav path, locomotion, attack selection, hit event, damage, death, and save-load.

defects.title: Animals animate in place instead of following idle routes
symptoms: Animals move their bodies but not through the world
Authored idle and wander routes do not complete
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW package, path, transform, root-motion, idle-marker, and loop timing differential passes for Goodsprings animals.

defects.title: Robots rotate incorrectly and interaction alignment spins or never settles
symptoms: Robot turning is misaligned
Interaction can spin indefinitely
Target alignment does not converge
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW robot controller, root axis, turn state, interaction target, and activation trace converges for representative securitrons.

defects.title: Birds do not align to authored perch targets
symptoms: Birds are displaced from boxes and expected targets
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW reference transform, target marker, controller, and animation-root trace matches every representative perch.

defects.title: Actors can be absent after load or cell transition
symptoms: Town can appear evacuated
Trudy is not reliably present
Different launch paths produce different actor populations
defects.requiredGate: Natural new-game, save-load, door, fast-travel, and VR launch matrices match retail winning references, enable state, persistent state, cell attachment, and actor count.

defects.title: Vault and Strip transitions can be missing or inaccessible
symptoms: A vault entrance lacked a usable door
The Strip could not be entered
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW crawl of every load door and teleport reference validates mesh, activation, lock, condition, quest gate, destination, return path, and persistence.

defects.title: Goodsprings actors appear stuck in incorrect quest and dialogue state
symptoms: Many actors repeat Powder Ganger or Khan topics
Dialogue availability does not match expected progression
Package state appears quest-stuck
defects.requiredGate: Natural Goodsprings quest spine matches retail stages, objectives, variables, conditions, topics, results, package overrides, rewards, save-load, and alternate branches.

defects.title: Displayed response text and selectable topic text do not reliably correspond
symptoms: Right-side dialogue does not work
Text on the right can differ from the option selected on the left
defects.requiredGate: Every Goodsprings speaker/topic passes retail differential for conditions, prompt, response, voice, result script, next topics, and UI selection identity.

defects.title: Pip-Boy, inventory buttons, and barter inventory are incomplete or empty
symptoms: Pip-Boy controls do not reliably respond
Barter panes can be empty
Inventory interaction is unstable
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW permutation covers every pane, tab, button, sort/filter, item action, barter mutation, caps transfer, quest item, save-load, flat input, and VR pointer input.

defects.title: VR hands may render while grip pointer, menu raycast, button mapping, and menu shaders fail
symptoms: Grip pointer can be invisible
Menus can render purple
Clicks and Escape mappings can fail
A menu may become impossible to close
defects.requiredGate: Mads OpenMW-VR hand and pointer behavior remains intact while every native FNV menu passes visible-ray, hover, click, close, shader, and input mapping tests in headset.

defects.title: Combat does not complete from attack through damage and death
symptoms: Mobs become angry but do not attack
Player attacks do not provide proven hit, damage, reaction, or death behavior
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW matrix covers melee, hitscan, projectile, ammo, armor, limb damage, stagger, hit reaction, AI pursuit, death, loot, XP, save-load, and representative creatures/NPCs.

defects.title: Fallout ambience, footsteps, combat, equipment, radio, and world sounds are largely absent
symptoms: Only some doors and voices are audible
World is otherwise nearly silent
defects.requiredGate: Retail/OpenMW event and waveform matrix resolves only FNV SOUN/SNDR/REGN/WTHR/material/animation sources for ambience, footsteps, combat, equipment, UI, radio, music, creatures, and activators.

defects.title: Morrowind sound fallback must never run during Fallout: New Vegas
symptoms: Risk of silently substituting ESM3 sound records for unresolved FNV events
defects.requiredGate: Runtime telemetry and source invariant prove zero ESM3 sound lookup or playback while the active game is FNV; every unresolved FNV event fails explicitly into the ledger.

defects.title: Excessive telemetry and graphics load make movement choppy or nearly impossible
symptoms: Continuous diagnostic chatter
Severe frame pacing and input degradation
High graphics configuration overwhelmed the machine
defects.requiredGate: Release-profile flat and VR traversal meets frame-time budgets with bounded asynchronous telemetry, no per-frame info logging, and no graphics mod requirement.

defects.title: Load can place the player in an invalid pit or produce divergent world state
symptoms: A rerun loaded into a weird pit
Actor population and systems differ between launch paths
defects.requiredGate: New game and representative save matrix validates player form, cell, transform, quest state, actor persistence, weather, inventory, packages, and VR state across repeated cold starts.

defects.title: Hidden lockstep runner timed out before scene state could converge
symptoms: Thirty initialization frames consumed the 30-second wall-clock timeout
Hour and weather commands were issued immediately before timeout
defects.requiredGate: The corrected four-case smoke completes all retail/OpenMW barriers and produces correlated screenshots plus state payloads before scaling to 10,000 cases.
